mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the equity discount rate and supports multiples, so continued month-over-month declines in policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines do not produce higher 20-day SPX returns than periods with flat or rising FEDFUNDS, or if the sign of the return effect is negative in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates sharply, real rates rise or tightening expectations increase, which compresses valuation multiples and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises by more than 0.4% month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if CPI-triggered episodes fail to show below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if inflation spikes are followed by positive returns in most triggered cases over any 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals labor market deterioration and weaker household income growth, which lowers near-term equity earnings expectations and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-rise triggers are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal loses directional accuracy for more than one full rolling year.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates improving payroll income and retail spending, which boosts near-term demand expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-RSAFS improvement signal does not outperform either series alone in predicting 20-day returns, or if triggered instances do not beat the unconditional return mean over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent declines in INDPRO indicate industrial production contraction and weaker corporate volume growth, which reduces earnings expectations and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if multi-month INDPRO declines do not coincide with below-zero 60-day SPX returns, or if the rolling correlation between INDPRO trend and forward returns turns positive for a sustained 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST from a depressed base signals easing financial conditions and future demand stabilization, which predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 5% over 3 months after a prior 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST rebound episodes are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than negative ones, or if the effect disappears when FEDFUNDS is unchanged.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and a stable or falling UNRATE signals genuine labor market improvement rather than headline noise, which strengthens earnings expectations and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-not-up signal fails to improve 20-day return hit rate versus each proxy alone, or if positive returns are absent in a majority of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, rising FEDFUNDS combined with accelerating CPIAUCSL compresses real liquidity and raises recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises while CPIAUCSL inflation is above its prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if tightening-plus-high-inflation episodes do not produce negative 5-day SPX returns, or if the combined interaction loses sign consistency in any 18-month rolling window.
